name: Business Description
question_text: Provide a brief history of the business, including the year established and ownership history.

name: Competitive Advantage
question_text: Describe the business' value proposition and core competitive advantage(s).

name: Sources of Revenue
question_text: Explain how the business makes money, including a breakdown of revenue by product/service, the split between transactional and contracted business, and any key contracts, if applicable.

name: Licensing Requirements
question_text: Does the business require any licenses or permits to operate? How will you meet these requirements?

name: Retail - Product Mix
question_text: What is your core product mix, and what makes your store/brand distinctive in the market?

name: Manufacturing - Products & Advantage
question_text: What do you manufacture and why do customers choose you (quality, lead time, customization, price, reliability)?

name: Restaurant - Concept & Positioning
question_text: What is your concept (service style, cuisine, positioning), and what makes it stand out in your local market?

name: Construction Contracting - Specialization
question_text: What types of projects do you specialize in, and what is your "sweet spot" (job size, customer type, trade niche)?

name: Hotel - Demand Drivers
question_text: What demand drivers support this property (business travel, events, tourism, hospitals/universities, highways), and how stable are they?

name: Healthcare - Services & Differentiator
question_text: What services do you provide and what is your "specialty" or differentiator in your market?

name: Manufacturing - Production Process
question_text: What does your production process look like at a high level, and where are the current bottlenecks or constraints?

name: Healthcare - Team Structure
question_text: What does your team structure look like (providers, support staff, admin), and how do you ensure consistent service quality?

name: Hotel - Operations Management
question_text: Who will manage day-to-day operations (owner, experienced GM, third-party manager), and why is that the right approach?

name: Retail - Target Customer
question_text: Who is your target customer, and what customer problem or preference are you best at serving?

name: Construction Contracting - Work Sourcing
question_text: How do you source new work (repeat clients, bid work, referrals, GCs, online leads), and what's working best right now?

name: Restaurant - Team Experience
question_text: What relevant experience do you and your key team members have in restaurant operations?

name: Manufacturing - Quality Controls
question_text: How do you ensure consistent quality (process controls, training, inspection, rework management)?

name: Retail - Sales Channels
question_text: How did you choose your sales channels (brick-and-mortar, e-commerce, omnichannel), and what is your plan to drive traffic?

name: Restaurant - Location Selection
question_text: How did you choose your location, and what about the surrounding area supports demand (daypart traffic, nearby anchors, competition)?

name: Healthcare - Client Generation
question_text: How do you generate new clients/patients (referrals, marketing, relationships), and what sustains repeat business?

name: Hotel - Value Proposition
question_text: What is the property's value proposition—why will guests choose it over alternatives (brand, location, amenities, pricing, condition)?

name: Construction Contracting - Job Selection
question_text: What criteria do you use to decide which jobs to take (profitability, risk, schedule fit, payment terms, customer quality)?

name: Retail - Inventory Management
question_text: How do you plan merchandise and manage inventory so you're not overstocked or understocked (process, tools, discipline)?

name: Healthcare - Scheduling & Capacity
question_text: What is your approach to scheduling and capacity (appointment flow, wait times, utilization, client experience)?

name: Construction Contracting - Cost Control
question_text: How do you estimate and control job costs (process, tools, change order discipline, job-cost reviews)?

name: Restaurant - Operating Model
question_text: What is your operating model (hours, staffing approach, dine-in vs takeout/delivery mix), and how does it fit local demand?

name: Hotel - Improvements
question_text: What improvements (if any) are planned to enhance guest experience or competitiveness, and how will you execute without major disruption?

name: Manufacturing - Supply Chain
question_text: How would you describe your supply chain reliability and key dependencies (materials availability, vendor concentration, lead times)?

name: Manufacturing - Workforce
question_text: What workforce skills are most critical, and what is your plan to recruit/retain/train for those needs?

name: Retail - Customer Strategy
question_text: What is your approach to customer acquisition and retention (promotions, loyalty, community events, social/digital strategy)?

name: Restaurant - Operational Controls
question_text: How will you control key operational risks (labor scheduling, food cost management, waste/spoilage, consistency/quality)?

name: Construction Contracting - Labor Management
question_text: How do you manage labor constraints (hiring, retention, subcontractor bench, scheduling to avoid overcommitment)?

name: Healthcare - Billing & Collections
question_text: How do you manage billing/collections processes operationally (workflow discipline, follow-up cadence, patient/client communication)?

name: Hotel - Bookings Strategy
question_text: How will you generate bookings (brand distribution, OTAs, direct bookings, corporate accounts), and what is your marketing approach?

name: Healthcare - Technology & Systems
question_text: What technology or systems are most important to your practice (practice management, EHR/POS, CRM), and are any changes planned?

name: Construction Contracting - Cash Flow
question_text: How do you manage cash flow timing (billing cadence, collections discipline, retainage, material deposits, vendor terms)?

name: Restaurant - Marketing & Retention
question_text: What is your plan to drive awareness and repeat business (marketing channels, loyalty, reviews, community partnerships)?

name: Hotel - Staffing Challenges
question_text: How will you manage staffing challenges and service consistency (hiring, training, retention, housekeeping reliability)?

name: Retail - Competitive Response
question_text: How will you respond if competitors discount aggressively or customer tastes shift (assortment agility, supplier flexibility, pricing strategy)?

name: Manufacturing - Demand Fluctuations
question_text: How do you handle fluctuations in demand (flex capacity, scheduling, overtime strategy, inventory buffers, diversification)?

name: Hotel - Market Risks
question_text: What are the biggest risks in this market (seasonality, new supply, local economic swings, online reviews), and how will you mitigate them?

name: Restaurant - Pressures & Response
question_text: What are the biggest pressures you anticipate (labor availability, competition, price sensitivity, seasonality), and how will you respond?

name: Healthcare - Practice Risks
question_text: What are the biggest risks in your practice (provider dependence, staffing shortages, reputation/reviews, referral concentration), and how do you mitigate them?

name: Manufacturing - Operational Risks
question_text: What are your biggest operational risks (equipment downtime, customer concentration, input cost swings), and how do you mitigate them?

name: Construction Contracting - Business Risks
question_text: What are the biggest risks for your business model (customer concentration, large-project exposure, warranty/callbacks, safety incidents), and how do you mitigate them?

name: Retail - Operational Risks
question_text: What are the main operational risks (shrinkage, staffing, seasonality, returns), and what controls do you have in place?

name: Business Acquisition - Attraction
question_text: What attracted you to this business specifically, and what makes it a good fit for you/your team?

name: Business Acquisition - Alternative Targets
question_text: If you evaluated multiple acquisition targets, what made you choose this one over the alternatives?

name: Business Acquisition - Success Vision
question_text: What does success look like in the first 90 days and the first year after closing?

name: Business Acquisition - Seller Role
question_text: What role has the seller played historically, and how will you replace that role day-one and long-term?

name: Business Acquisition - Post-Close Operations
question_text: Who will run day-to-day operations post-close, and what are the responsibilities of each key leader?

name: Business Acquisition - Relationship Retention
question_text: What are the most important relationships to preserve (employees, key customers, vendors, referral partners), and what is your retention plan?

name: Business Acquisition - Due Diligence Risks
question_text: What are the top risks you identified in diligence (operational, people, market, systems), and what specific steps will you take to mitigate them?

name: CRE Construction - Project Scope
question_text: Describe the project scope in plain language—what are you building or improving, and why is it important to the business?

name: CRE Construction - Current Stage
question_text: What stage are you in today (concept, plans/design, permitting, contractor selection, ready to start), and what's left before work begins?

name: CRE Construction - Contractor Selection
question_text: Have you identified a contractor/GC (or how will you select one), and what gives you confidence in their ability to deliver?

name: CRE Construction - Bids & Estimates
question_text: Have you received detailed bids/estimates, and how did you validate the scope (apples-to-apples comparisons, allowances, exclusions)?

name: CRE Construction - Timeline
question_text: What is your expected timeline and critical milestones (start, major phases, completion, opening/ramp), and what could cause delays?

name: CRE Construction - Project Management
question_text: Who is managing the project day-to-day on your side, and how will you monitor progress, budget discipline, and change orders?

name: CRE Construction - Cost Contingency
question_text: If costs run higher or timelines slip, what is your contingency plan (scope adjustments, phased buildout, interim operations, alternative vendors)?

name: CRE Improvements - Project Scope
question_text: Describe the project scope in plain language—what are you building or improving, and why is it important to the business?

name: CRE Improvements - Current Stage
question_text: What stage are you in today (concept, plans/design, permitting, contractor selection, ready to start), and what's left before work begins?

name: CRE Improvements - Contractor Selection
question_text: Have you identified a contractor/GC (or how will you select one), and what gives you confidence in their ability to deliver?

name: CRE Improvements - Bids & Estimates
question_text: Have you received detailed bids/estimates, and how did you validate the scope (apples-to-apples comparisons, allowances, exclusions)?

name: CRE Improvements - Timeline
question_text: What is your expected timeline and critical milestones (start, major phases, completion, opening/ramp), and what could cause delays?

name: CRE Improvements - Project Management
question_text: Who is managing the project day-to-day on your side, and how will you monitor progress, budget discipline, and change orders?

name: CRE Improvements - Cost Contingency
question_text: If costs run higher or timelines slip, what is your contingency plan (scope adjustments, phased buildout, interim operations, alternative vendors)?

name: CRE Purchase - Strategic Rationale
question_text: Why does owning this property make sense for your business strategy versus continuing to lease?

name: CRE Purchase - Location Selection
question_text: What drew you to this specific location (customer access, workforce, logistics, visibility, competitors, long-term viability)?

name: CRE Purchase - Operational Fit
question_text: How does the property fit your operational needs today and your growth plans over the next several years?

name: CRE Purchase - Due Diligence
question_text: What due diligence have you done on the property's condition and suitability (layout, functionality, known issues, constraints)?

name: CRE Purchase - Improvements Needed
question_text: What improvements (if any) are needed to make it operational for your business, and how do you plan to execute them with minimal disruption?

name: CRE Purchase - Excess Space
question_text: If any space is not immediately needed for your operations, what is your plan for that space (future growth, tenants, storage, etc.)?

name: CRE Purchase - Backup Plan
question_text: If closing is delayed or the property needs more work than expected, what is your backup plan to keep business plans on track?

name: Debt Refinance - Motivation
question_text: What prompted the refinance at this point (strategic reset, simplify structure, improve flexibility, remove pressure points, etc.)?

name: Debt Refinance - Operational Impact
question_text: How has the existing debt impacted the business operationally (decision-making, growth, cash planning, stress points)?

name: Debt Refinance - Post-Refinance Benefits
question_text: What will be different after the refinance—what does it enable you to do (or stop doing)?

name: Debt Refinance - Breathing Room
question_text: If refinancing reduces monthly pressure, how do you plan to use that breathing room (stabilize operations, reinvest, build reserves, other)?

name: Debt Refinance - Discipline Changes
question_text: What operational or financial discipline changes have you made (or will you make) to avoid returning to the same situation?

name: Debt Refinance - Process Complexities
question_text: Are there any complexities you anticipate in the payoff/refi process (multiple lenders, liens, documentation gaps), and how will you manage them?

name: Debt Refinance - Remaining Risks
question_text: What risks remain after the refinance, and how will you monitor and respond if performance softens?

name: Equipment - Problem Solved
question_text: What problem does this equipment solve or what capability does it add (capacity, speed, quality, new offering, cost savings)?

name: Equipment - Selection Rationale
question_text: Why did you choose this specific equipment/vendor, and what alternatives did you consider?

name: Equipment - Implementation Plan
question_text: What is the implementation plan (ordering, delivery, installation, downtime, testing), and who is accountable for making it successful?

name: Equipment - Staffing & Training
question_text: What staffing or training is required to operate and maintain it effectively?

name: Equipment - Maintenance Plan
question_text: What is your plan for service/maintenance support (warranties, local technicians, spare parts, backup options)?

name: Equipment - Operational Impact
question_text: How will this change your operations or customer experience (turnaround time, reliability, ability to take larger jobs, etc.)?

name: Equipment - Slower Benefits
question_text: If benefits take longer to materialize than expected, what adjustments would you make (utilization strategy, pricing, sales focus, cost controls)?

name: Existing Business - Business Description
question_text: Briefly describe the business today—what you do, who you serve, and what most drives demand for your product/service.

name: Existing Business - Recent Changes
question_text: What have been the most important changes in the business over the last 12–24 months (wins, challenges, or shifts), and what caused them?

name: Existing Business - Strengths
question_text: What are the biggest strengths of the business (reputation, relationships, location, brand, process, pricing power, etc.)?

name: Existing Business - Operational Constraints
question_text: What are the biggest operational constraints today (labor, space, capacity, supply chain, scheduling, technology), and how are you addressing them?

name: Existing Business - Customer Retention
question_text: How do you win and retain customers today? What tends to make a customer choose you over alternatives?

name: Existing Business - Key Person Dependencies
question_text: Describe the management structure and any "key-person dependencies." If one key person stepped away for 30 days, what would break, and how are you reducing that risk?

name: Existing Business - Future Priorities
question_text: Looking ahead 12–18 months, what are your top priorities and what milestones would you like to achieve?

name: Expansion - Description
question_text: Describe the expansion in practical terms (new location, added capacity, new service line, expanded territory, additional shifts, etc.).

name: Expansion - Timing
question_text: Why is now the right time to expand—what's the opportunity you're responding to?

name: Expansion - Demand Confidence
question_text: What gives you confidence the expanded business will be supported by demand (customer requests, backlog, market gap, competitive advantage)?

name: Expansion - Execution Plan
question_text: What is your execution plan—who is responsible for making the expansion happen and keeping the core business running smoothly?

name: Expansion - Staffing
question_text: What staffing changes are needed (hiring, training, leadership coverage), and how will you ensure you have the right team in place?

name: Expansion - Marketing
question_text: How will you market/sell the expanded offering (new customers, upsell existing customers, partnerships, pricing/positioning changes)?

name: Expansion - Risks
question_text: What could derail the expansion (timing, hiring, cost, supply chain, competition), and what contingencies do you have?

name: Franchise - Model Choice
question_text: Why did you choose a franchise model versus an independent concept, and why this brand in particular?

name: Franchise - Owner Role
question_text: What does your role look like—how hands-on will you be, and who is the operator/GM responsible for execution?

name: Franchise - Franchisor Support
question_text: What kind of support are you expecting from the franchisor (training, marketing, site selection, operations), and how will you use it?

name: Franchise - Location Fit
question_text: Why is your location/territory a strong fit for the brand (customer demographics, traffic patterns, competition, local demand)?

name: Franchise - Local Marketing
question_text: How will you execute marketing locally while staying aligned with the brand (community partnerships, local promos, digital strategy)?

name: Franchise - Staffing & Training
question_text: What is your staffing and training plan to deliver the brand experience consistently?

name: Franchise - Launch Risks
question_text: What are the biggest risks to a successful launch or ramp (buildout timing, hiring, brand saturation, local competitors), and how will you mitigate them?

name: Inventory - Purchase Purpose
question_text: What inventory are you purchasing, and what is the business reason (seasonality, larger orders, new product line, bulk buying, supply risk)?

name: Inventory - Sell-Through Confidence
question_text: What gives you confidence you can sell through the additional inventory (sales channels, customer demand, historical patterns)?

name: Inventory - Supplier Reliability
question_text: How reliable are your suppliers right now, and what is your plan if lead times or availability change?

name: Inventory - Storage & Protection
question_text: How will you store, manage, and protect the inventory (space, handling, shrinkage controls, temperature/special requirements)?

name: Inventory - Reorder Management
question_text: How do you decide reorder points and avoid overbuying—what internal process keeps inventory "healthy"?

name: Inventory - Obsolescence Risk
question_text: What are the risks of obsolescence/spoilage/returns in your product mix, and how do you mitigate them?

name: Inventory - Demand Softening
question_text: If demand softens or sell-through is slower than expected, what is your contingency plan (discounting, channel shifts, bundling, return agreements)?

name: Partner Buyout - Driving Reason
question_text: What is driving the buyout (retirement, strategic change, misalignment, other), and why is this the best path forward?

name: Partner Buyout - Role Coverage
question_text: How were roles and responsibilities divided previously, and how will those duties be covered after the buyout?

name: Partner Buyout - Governance
question_text: How will decision-making and governance work post-buyout (who owns what decisions, and how will disagreements be handled)?

name: Partner Buyout - Relationship Continuity
question_text: Are any customer/vendor/employee relationships tied closely to the departing partner—and how will you maintain continuity?

name: Partner Buyout - Visible Changes
question_text: What changes (if any) will employees, customers, or vendors notice after the buyout, and how will you communicate those changes?

name: Partner Buyout - Strategic Improvements
question_text: What operational improvements or strategic shifts do you plan to make once the buyout is complete?

name: Partner Buyout - Buyout Risks
question_text: What are the biggest risks of the buyout (people, reputation, disruption, morale), and how will you reduce those risks?

name: Start Up - Motivation
question_text: What motivated you to start this business, and why is now the right time to launch it?

name: Start Up - Differentiation
question_text: What problem are you solving, and what makes your offering meaningfully different from competitors?

name: Start Up - Demand Evidence
question_text: What evidence do you have that customers will buy (examples: prior experience, signed interest, pilot customers, industry relationships, market testing)?

name: Start Up - Go-to-Market
question_text: Who is your target customer and how will you reach them in the first 3–6 months (your go-to-market approach)?

name: Start Up - Early Milestones
question_text: What are the most important early milestones (launch, staffing, vendor setup, first customers, break-even progress), and what's your plan to hit them?

name: Start Up - Partners & Permits
question_text: What key partners, vendors, or permits/licensing steps are required to operate—and what's your status on those items?

name: Start Up - Launch Risks
question_text: What are the biggest risks to a successful launch (execution, hiring, timing, competition), and what is your contingency plan if the ramp is slower than expected?

name: Transition Risk - Nature of Change
question_text: What exactly is changing, and why does that create transition risk (ownership, leadership, customer relationships, processes, location, systems)?

name: Transition Risk - Key Responsibilities
question_text: Which responsibilities or relationships are most tied to the person/entity leaving, and how will those be transferred?

name: Transition Risk - Timeline
question_text: What is the timeline for the transition, and what are the "must-hit" handoff milestones?

name: Transition Risk - Knowledge Transfer
question_text: What knowledge transfer will occur (documentation, training, introductions, overlap period), and who owns that plan?

name: Transition Risk - Employee Alignment
question_text: How will you keep employees confident and aligned during the transition (communication, incentives, leadership coverage)?

name: Transition Risk - Stakeholder Retention
question_text: How will you reassure and retain key customers/vendors/referral sources during the transition?

name: Transition Risk - Fallback Plan
question_text: If the transition takes longer than expected—or a key person leaves unexpectedly—what is your fallback plan?

name: Working Capital - Practical Use
question_text: What will the working capital be used for in practical terms (timing gaps, payroll, ramping a new initiative, marketing, stabilizing operations)?

name: Working Capital - Driving Need
question_text: What is driving the working capital need (growth opportunity, seasonality, one-time transition, customer payment timing, other)?

name: Working Capital - New Actions
question_text: What will you do differently with this liquidity (specific actions or investments you haven't been able to make)?

name: Working Capital - Tracking & Management
question_text: How will you track and manage the working capital so it supports the intended goal (budgeting, checkpoints, owner oversight)?

name: Working Capital - Operational Improvements
question_text: What operational improvements are you implementing to reduce ongoing cash strain (pricing, collections processes, cost structure, staffing model)?

name: Working Capital - Other Flexibility
question_text: What other sources of flexibility do you have or plan to build (vendor terms, deposits, retainer model, scheduling changes)?

name: Working Capital - Future Liquidity
question_text: If you still need additional liquidity later, what is your plan (scaling back growth, prioritizing expenses, pivoting sales strategy, other)?
